Judge will verify the fork and replay commands work as documented and outputs are durable across the reset.

input.feature: Loop iterations carry learned facts via memory without re-computation
input.task: Build a <Loop> workflow that iteratively fixes bugs: (1) 'identify-bug' task (agent) scans for a bug and stores the fix pattern via memory={{ remember: { key: `bug-${iteration}:pattern` } }} with a 30-day TTL. (2) 'apply-fix' task (agent) uses memory={{ recall: { topK: 5 } }} to see all prior patterns and applies the best one. Loop until no more bugs (or max 5 iterations). On each iteration, verify that patterns from iteration N are available to iteration N+1. Return { bugsFixed, patternsDiscovered, iterationCount }. Run the workflow twice in the same workflow namespace and verify the second run has fewer iterations because it recalled patterns from the first run.

verify.rubric: Judge must verify: (1) Loop runs multiple iterations; (2) first task in loop stores a fact with key that includes the bug id or pattern (keyed so facts don't overwrite); (3) second task recalls facts with topK and demonstrates using them in its prompt; (4) facts survive loop iteration boundaries (they're in the store, not in task output); (5) first complete run stores 4-5 pattern facts; (6) second run starting fresh (or resume of same workflow) reads the stored patterns from first run via recall and uses them to fix bugs faster (fewer iterations); (7) output includes iterationCount and patternsDiscovered count. Candidate must show both runs and prove second run had faster convergence.
